frameworks.name: transcranial direct current stimulation (tDCS)
frameworks.one_line: anodal cortical stimulation modulates V1 excitability to enhance perceptual function

frameworks.name: cortical magnification
frameworks.one_line: central visual field is over-represented in V1, predicting stronger stimulation effects

frameworks.name: neural plasticity
frameworks.one_line: repetitive noninvasive stimulation produces lasting changes in visual cortex function

core_question: Can repetitive anodal tDCS over V1 produce lasting improvements in contrast sensitivity in healthy adults?
key_assumptions: anodal tDCS increases cortical excitability in targeted V1
contrast sensitivity is a core V1-mediated function
current density and cortical magnification jointly shape stimulation effects
manipulations: repeated daily sessions across 5 consecutive days
visual field eccentricity of contrast threshold measurement
key_findings: contrast sensitivity improved from the second day of anodal tDCS onward
improvement effects lasted 24 hours between sessions
anodal group showed larger gains than sham after the final stimulation (23 vs 5%)
long-term effects at 4 weeks were confined to the central 2-4 degrees of the visual field
interpretations: repetitive anodal tDCS over V1 can produce lasting enhancement of visual perception
central-field specificity reflects higher current density and cortical magnification of central retina
